display_info.name: Sagitta Star Tracker
display_info.description: Provides arc-second range pointing accuracy for CubeSats; custom algorithms optimized for accuracy, robustness and low computational cost; built-in baffle for stray light reduction; compact and easy to interface for CubeSats and large satellites; tracks stars up to magnitude 6, up to 32 stars; lost-in-space availability >99% of the night sky; baffle sun rejection angle 40 deg; lens is radiation shielded; TRL 8

excel_and_cad.excel_description: Provides arc-second range pointing accuracy for CubeSats; custom algorithms optimized for accuracy, robustness and low computational cost; built-in baffle for stray light reduction; compact and easy to interface for CubeSats and large satellites; tracks stars up to magnitude 6, up to 32 stars; lost-in-space availability >99% of the night sky; baffle sun rejection angle 40 deg; lens is radiation shielded; TRL 8

display_info.name: AQUILA-H01 Sun Sensor
display_info.name_cn: 太阳敏感器 AQUILA-H01 Sun Sensor
display_info.description: A sun sensor determines a spacecraft’s orientation with respect to the sun. The front surface of the NewSpace Systems (Aquila) sun sensor is a synthetic sapphire window with a reflective metal coating beneath it. Slits are etched in the metal, and sunlight passes through them and an optical filter onto a sensor. The charge on the photo sensors is read by the microcontroller, which processes the image and computes the sun vector. The vector and other telemetry are returned to the spacecraft through the serial interface. The unit can be powered from unregulated DC power from the spacecraft.
display_info.material: Aluminum housing + optical window + silicon photodiodes + PCB

excel_and_cad.excel_name_cn: 太阳敏感器 AQUILA-H01 Sun Sensor
excel_and_cad.excel_description: A sun sensor determines a spacecraft’s orientation with respect to the sun. The front surface of the NewSpace Systems (Aquila) sun sensor is a synthetic sapphire window with a reflective metal coating beneath it. Slits are etched in the metal, and sunlight passes through them and an optical filter onto a sensor. The charge on the photo sensors is read by the microcontroller, which processes the image and computes the sun vector. excel_and_cad.excel_material: Aluminum housing + optical window + silicon photodiodes + PCB

display_info.name: PEGASUS Magnetometer
display_info.description: The NSS tri-axial magnetometer utilizes Anisotropic Magneto-Resistive (AMR) sensors, which are co-located with offset compensating circuitry. The offset compensating circuitry nulls the characteristic offset voltage of the AMR sensor, which enhances the sensor performance. The sensor provides x-, y- and z-axes magnetic field component measurements, as well as a sensor temperature measurement which is used for the temperature compensation of the magnetic field measurement. This product includes low noise, precision processing, and analog-to-digital conversion circuitry which improves its linearity and reduces the drift sensitivity of the sensor head. The integrated processing circuitry and sensor head provide the mission with an accurate and stable magnetic field measurement at low power consumption. The power requirement is a regulated 5 V DC supply while the measured x-, y- and z-axes magnetic field components are available on a digital serial interface.
display_info.material: Aluminum alloy housing + PCB + 3-axis magnetic sensor assembly

excel_and_cad.excel_description: The NSS tri-axial magnetometer utilizes Anisotropic Magneto-Resistive (AMR) sensors, which are co-located with offset compensating circuitry. The offset compensating circuitry nulls the characteristic offset voltage of the AMR sensor, which enhances the sensor performance. The sensor provides x-, y- and z-axes magnetic field component measurements, as well as a sensor temperature measurement which is used for the temperature compensation of the magnetic field measurement. This product includes low noise, precision processing, and analog-to-digital conversion circuitry which improves its linearity and reduces the drift sensitivity of the sensor head. The integrated processing circuitry and sensor head provide the mission with an accurate and stable magnetic field measurement at low power consumption. The power requirement is a regulated 5 V DC supply while the measured x-, y- and z-axes magnetic field components are available on a digital serial interface.

excel_and_cad.excel_description: L1 frequency GPS receiver utilizing heritage GPS chipset; for low-cost SmallSat constellations to provide orbit position/velocity, time knowledge, orbit maneuvers and payload data stamping; radiation tolerant COTS, simple interface, flight heritage; improved from NGPS-01-422, supports NMEA083 and Binary protocol

display_info.name: NSGY-001 Stellar Gyro
display_info.description: The NewSpace NSGY-001 Stellar Gyro can be used to propagate a spacecraft's attitude from a known initial condition, without drift, while sufficient stars are common across frames. The image-based rotation estimates can complement a set of MEMS rate gyroscopes to maintain a high accuracy attitude estimate at low angular rates (where MEMS gyroscope drift is most severe). The NewSpace stellar gyroscope uses a COTS sensor and optics resulting in a low-cost attitude determination system that maintains accuracy during the eclipse phase. It can achieve this by using algorithms that tolerate noise and does not require a star database. It is thus far more robust against radiation damage than a standard star mapper solution would be if based on the same components. The Stellar Gyro has passed through qualification testing and is due for first launch in 2020. Key features of the NSS stellar gyroscope include: Active pixel CMOS detector Small size and low mass No baffle required Low power Simple to interface Immune to Moon and Earth in FoV The NSS stellar gyroscope can be used in the following applications: High performance 3-axis rate sensor Full sky sensor for agile satellites
display_info.material: Aluminum housing + optics + CMOS detector + PCB

excel_and_cad.excel_description: The NewSpace NSGY-001 Stellar Gyro can be used to propagate a spacecraft's attitude from a known initial condition, without drift, while sufficient stars are common across frames. The image-based rotation estimates can complement a set of MEMS rate gyroscopes to maintain a high accuracy attitude estimate at low angular rates (where MEMS gyroscope drift is most severe). The NewSpace stellar gyroscope uses a COTS sensor and optics resulting in a low-cost attitude determination system that maintains accuracy during the eclipse phase. display_info.name: Water-Powered Electrothermal CubeSat Propulsion System
display_info.name_cn: 水工质电热式 CubeSat 推进系统
display_info.description: Fully integrated, flight-proven electrothermal thruster specifically engineered for CubeSat missions; installed outside the main satellite structure within standard ⌀80 mm CubeSat deployer tuna-can volume to maximize internal payload space; uses low-pressure non-toxic water as green propellant, eliminating risks of high-pressure/toxic fuels; integrated with thruster head, propellant tank, heat exchanger and control electronics; applied for collision avoidance, de-orbiting, mission life extension, constellation management, orbit phasing, stationkeeping and critical orbit correction; flight heritage since 2022; qualified per ESA ECSS-E-ST-10-03C and NASA GSFC-STD-7000A; ITAR-Free; no RF interference; supports water-alcohol mixture for low-temperature operation
display_info.material: 3D printed aerospace-grade AlSi10Mg aluminum alloy structure & tank + stainless steel thruster head + PEEK insulation + FR-4 PCB control electronics
